<!-- Generated by the atlas-deep-analysis multi-agent workflow (6 dimension analysts + synthesis), 2026-06-01. -->

# Sugi Atlas — Improvement Roadmap

Synthesis of six dimension analyses (Presentation/UX, AI/RAG-Readiness, SEO, Unique-Content, Competitive, Per-Entity Depth). Evidence anchors reference the pages/docs the analysts cited.

## Framing

Atlas's hard-to-fake assets are already in place: clean static HTML with no JS gate, inline schema.org JSON-LD with federated `sameAs` identifier closure, per-section `provenance.json` with replayable biobtree chains, machine sidecars (`entity.jsonld` / `provenance.json` / `bundle.json`), and depth no single competitor pairs (UniProt+NCBI narrative AND ClinVar/AlphaMissense/SpliceAI/TF/CIViC on one page). The gene-narrative gap that `03_page_audit.md` flagged is now **closed**.

The problems are concentrated in three places: (1) the *renderer/theme plumbing* that converts those assets into human readability and search ranking; (2) a *published-state defect* — the LLM `## Summary` exists only on drug pages, not gene/disease, despite `llms.txt` promising it on every page; (3) the *disease entity*, which the original audit never covered and which carries dead sections, false-positive data, and clinically nonsensical derived suggestions.

A recurring theme across all six lenses: **the best content is buried under raw dumps** (TP53 is 117,742 chars with a 313-row PDB table inline). The fix is rarely "collect more data" — it is reorder, collapse, synthesize, and cross-link what is already in the bundle.

---

## TRACK A — PRESENTATION (how pages look and read)

### Quick wins (low effort, high/medium impact)

1. **Add `description:` + SEO `title:` to frontmatter** *(effort: low)* — The perfect ~150-char definition already exists in the JSON-LD `description` / declarative lead but never reaches `<meta description>` or `<title>`. Titles are bare ("TP53") with inconsistent casing ("cardiomyopathy", "IMATINIB"). One-line change in `assemble_page` reusing already-computed declarative text. *(SEO, Competitive)*

2. **Wrap wide tables in horizontal-scroll container** *(effort: low, theme)* — 9-column disease variant tables and 6-column CIViC tables overflow on mobile. Add `.content table { overflow-x:auto }` wrapper / render-hook. *(Presentation)*

3. **Add a sticky table-of-contents in the theme** *(effort: low, theme)* — Hugo exposes `.TableOfContents` from H2/H3 for free. Gene pages have 14+ sections over 700–1400 lines with no way to jump to "Disease associations" without scrolling past 313 PDB rows. *(Presentation)*

4. **Round floats; suppress empty/teaser sections** *(effort: low)* — MAF `0.19679999999999997` reads as a bug; round to 3–4 sig figs. Sotorasib `## Pharmacogenomics` renders a header + dangling "...annotations for this drug:" with no content — gate empty sections out entirely (reuse gene-side empty-omission). *(Presentation, Per-Entity)*

5. **Fix the run-on subcellular-location string** *(effort: low)* — TP53 renders "Cytoplasm. Nucleus. Nucleus. PML body..." (isoform flags concatenated, undeduped). Parse/dedupe into a labeled list. This is exactly the one-line fact an LLM repeats. *(Competitive)*

6. **`<link rel="alternate">` head hints to sidecars** *(effort: low, theme)* — Add `entity.jsonld` / `provenance.json` / `bundle.json` discovery links so a crawler landing on HTML (not via `llms.txt`) finds the machine artifacts. *(RAG, SEO)*

### Strategic bets (higher effort, high impact)

7. **Wrap long tables/lists in `<details><summary>` collapsibles** *(effort: medium)* — `<details>` is used nowhere in the codebase (zero hits). Show count + top-N by default ("Experimental structures (PDB): 313 — show all"), full table inside the fold. Cuts visible TP53 length 3–5x while keeping all data for crawlers. Applies to PDB, miRNA (327 rows), bioactivity dumps, identifier/accession lists. *(Presentation, Unique-Content, Competitive)*

8. **Reorder gene/disease sections to "most-wanted answer first"** *(effort: medium)* — Currently: lead → CIViC cancer prose / identifiers → … → disease associations last; function prose is buried inside "Protein identifiers." Reorder to lead → summary → function/role → clinical/disease → drugs/evidence → then identifier/transcript/structure reference tables. Same fix demotes the promiscuous-compound table (NITROFURANTOIN/COLCHICINE) below the curated CIViC triple. *(Presentation, Competitive)*

9. **Wire the LLM executive `## Summary` onto gene + disease pages** *(effort: medium)* — *This straddles both tracks; see Track B #1.* Presentation impact: it's the "above-the-fold" answer to "what is this and why do I care," currently absent on 2 of 3 entity types.

10. **Minify the inline JSON-LD block** *(effort: low)* — Disease pages embed all 50 `associatedGene` objects inline, pushing the readable lead to source line ~593–750. Emit minified so the body starts near the top. No visible change. *(Presentation, Per-Entity)*

---

## TRACK B — UNIQUE CONTENT / DIFFERENTIATION

Each idea stress-tested below. **[REAL]** = feasible now under wrapper + open-license constraints. **[ASPIRATIONAL]** = needs new data, license, or upstream change.

### Quick wins (low effort, high impact)

1. **Ship the LLM executive `## Summary` on gene + disease pages — [REAL]** *(effort: medium)* — The single biggest published-state defect. The synthesis layer is built (drug pages have it), `llms.txt` advertises it on every page, but gene/disease were published with `--no-summary`. This is the one piece of cross-dataset synthesis no upstream source offers and exactly what an LLM lifts-and-cites. *Skeptic note: this is genuine synthesis, not re-rendering — keep the disclosure line.* *(RAG, Presentation, SEO)*

2. **Answer-first precision-oncology verdict from the CIViC triple — [REAL]** *(effort: low)* — Promote a 1–2 sentence deterministic synthesis of top Level-A/B triples into the page lead ("KRAS G12C confers sensitivity to sotorasib/adagrasib in NSCLC, CIViC Level A; KRAS exon-2 → cetuximab resistance in CRC"). The data is already on gene §10 / drug / disease §13 but buried at line ~1147. CC0 from CIViC. *Genuinely unique — no competitor assembles allele×drug×indication×evidence-level as a static citable line.* *(Unique-Content, Competitive, RAG)*

3. **Filter HuGE-Navigator boilerplate from GeneRIFs — [REAL]** *(effort: low)* — ~18 of 30 surfaced GeneRIFs are identical "Observational study of gene-disease association. (HuGE Navigator)" filler. Down-rank the template before taking top-N so the 30 shown are mechanistic, quotable, PMID-anchored claims. *(Presentation, RAG, Competitive)*

4. **Stable hand-authored section anchors + § numbering — [REAL]** *(effort: low)* — Headings are plain prose with fragile Hugo auto-slugs; `llms.txt` tells agents to cite `#disease-associations` but ampersand headings mangle. Emit explicit `{#s10}` anchors keyed to `provenance.json` section IDs so the citation contract is durable across regenerations. *(RAG)*

5. **Populate `biobtree_version` + dataset snapshot dates — [REAL]** *(effort: low)* — Every page shows `biobtree_version: "unknown"`, silently undercutting the #1-ranked moat (reproducible provenance). Wire the real version into frontmatter + sidecars + JSON-LD `version`/`dateModified`. Cheap; converts the provenance moat from aspirational to verifiable. *(RAG, Competitive, SEO)*

6. **Fix disease Pharmacogenomics false-positive "VIP:1" — [REAL]** *(effort: low)* — Every cohort gene (including non-pharmacogenes like ciliary dyneins) is flagged "VIP entries: 1" — almost certainly "present in PharmGKB" mislabeled as Very-Important-Pharmacogene (real VIP set is ~60 genes total). Relabel to "PharmGKB entry" and drop when CPIC=0. This actively misinforms AI consumers today. *(Per-Entity)*

7. **Suppress dead disease GWAS/variant-tier sections — [REAL]** *(effort: low)* — PCD/HBOC render "0 GWAS associations / 0 studies" and all-zero Tier 1–4 tables. Collapse when empty; add "Mendelian disorder — no common-variant GWAS signal" note. *Also verify the GWAS route isn't a routing miss* — BRCA1/2 do have GWAS hits, so HBOC zeros are suspicious. *(Per-Entity)*

8. **Disease definition line from Mondo/Orphanet `def` — [REAL]** *(effort: medium)* — Disease leads are bare count sentences ("a disease with 50 cohort genes and N trials"); no clinical definition. Mondo's own `def` annotation / Orphanet textual definition (CC-BY) is redistributable, no LLM needed. The disease analog of the UniProt-FUNCTION win for genes. *(Per-Entity, Competitive)*

### Strategic bets (high impact, higher effort)

9. **Build the internal cross-entity link mesh + self-closing JSON-LD graph — [REAL]** *(effort: medium)* — *Highest-leverage structural move.* Today every gene/disease/drug name links OUT to genenames.org/civicdb.org; the three entity types that share MONDO/HGNC/ChEMBL IDs are crawl-isolated, leaking all link equity offsite. The drug side already proves it's trivial (sotorasib's `entity.jsonld` carries `target` Gene + `treats` MedicalCondition). Add `associatedDisease`/`drug`/`bioChemInteraction` to gene JSON-LD, render "Related Atlas pages" blocks, and point `@id`/`url` at Atlas pages when the entity exists in the corpus. Makes Atlas the only public biomedical resource publishing a fully connected, dereferenceable cross-entity graph. *(Unique-Content, SEO, RAG, Competitive)*

10. **AlphaMissense × SpliceAI × ClinVar concordance shortlist — [REAL]** *(effort: medium)* — Atlas is the only page co-locating all three; currently three independent dumps. Join on position/HGVS and emit a derived "high-confidence damaging" shortlist + count ("38 variants where ClinVar Pathogenic, AlphaMissense >0.9, SpliceAI >0.5 all agree"). *Genuinely unique — no single source can compute it. Skeptic note: needs careful HGVS normalization across three coordinate conventions; the join is the real work, not the rendering.* *(Unique-Content, Per-Entity, RAG)*

11. **Sitemap + robots.txt + catalog `_index` pages — [REAL]** *(effort: medium, theme)* — No sitemap, no robots.txt, no crawl-entry catalog pages exist in dist. Every page is an orphan. Ship sitemap with `<lastmod>`, robots.txt allow-listing the AI user-agents, and `/atlas/`, `/atlas/gene/`, etc. index pages (which double as internal-link hubs). *(SEO)*

12. **Drug MoA + PK/ADME + safety narrative — [REAL, partial]** *(effort: medium)* — Drug pages lack mechanism-of-action prose, PK/ADME, and any safety surface. **[REAL]:** ChEMBL `mechanism_of_action` free-text (open) rendered like UniProt FUNCTION; openFDA label adverse-reactions (public domain); FAERS aggregate. **[ASPIRATIONAL]:** DrugBank's curated PK/dosing is non-commercial-gated — link-out only. *Verify what biobtree already exposes before filing ingest.* Also fix the `max_phase` artifact labeling FDA-approved imatinib as "0 indications at phase 4." *(Per-Entity, Competitive)*

13. **Emit a per-page `claims.jsonld` (atomic citeable assertions) — [REAL]** *(effort: high)* — One line per assertion: subject ID, predicate, object ID, evidence level, source dataset, source URL, biobtree chain (e.g. the KRAS-G12C→Sotorasib→NSCLC CIViC-A triple as one record). No upstream ships pre-chunked, provenance-stamped, cross-dataset claim records; this is the ideal RAG ingest unit. *Pure synthesis over the existing bundle — genuinely unique.* *(RAG, Unique-Content)*

14. **Gate disease repurposing/druggability synthesis on disease class — [REAL]** *(effort: medium)* — PCD (recessive ciliopathy) currently lists SORAFENIB/DASATINIB/ERLOTINIB as "repurposing opportunities" via off-target kinase binding — clinically nonsensical and potentially harmful if quoted by an AI. Gate on GenCC inheritance / intOGen-absence; require drug indication to overlap disease MONDO; reframe as "research-signal chemical tractability." Also flag cohort genes by evidence route (curated GenCC/Orphanet/OMIM = core vs ClinVar/network-only = peripheral) and run derived views over core only. This turns the noisiest liability into a transparency asset. *(Per-Entity, Competitive, Unique-Content)*

### Stress-test verdicts on the remaining proposed ideas

- **Per-gene druggability/tractability A–E tier** (fuse ChEMBL max-phase + PDB/AlphaFold + DepMap + family) — **[REAL]**, effort medium. The disease §16 pyramid machinery already exists; reuse it. Genuinely unique single-label verdict.
- **DepMap/ClinGen "essentiality/dosage verdict" sentence** — **[REAL]**, effort low. Data is already on the page (KRAS 52.4%); just synthesize into one answer-first line.
- **Drug-response matrix / "[gene] [variant] [drug] response" long-tail pages** — **[REAL]**, effort medium. Re-views existing CIViC triples into crawl-optimized surfaces — *valuable for SEO long-tail, but be honest: it's re-rendering existing facts in a new URL shape, not new content.* Worth it for the zero-competition query coverage.
- **Synthetic cross-entity hub pages** ("drugs targeting KRAS", "genes in disease X") — **[REAL]**, effort medium. Inverts existing bundle relationships; doubles as internal-link hubs.
- **Pathway/family aggregate/comparative pages** ("RAS-family drivers compared") — **[REAL but higher effort]**. `cohort.fan()` infra exists; genuinely cross-entity synthesis no single-source competitor can produce. Effort high.
- **Content-addressed fact fingerprints (immutable citation IDs)** — **[REAL]**, effort medium, but **lower priority** — only valuable once `claims.jsonld` exists.
- **Per-regeneration diff / changelog pages** — **[ASPIRATIONAL→REAL-later]**. Requires storing prior builds + a diff pipeline; real freshness/E-E-A-T payoff but no infra today. Defer.
- **Per-fact provenance (fact→source map)** — **[REAL but high effort]**. Requires pushing the `produces` tracking down from section to field level. High value for citeability; sequence after `claims.jsonld`.
- **CIViC triples into JSON-LD (`MedicalGuideline`/typed array)** — **[REAL]**, effort medium. The highest-value content currently lives only in a markdown table; putting it in the structured layer is where AI tools parse first.

---

## TOP 3 MOAT MOVES

1. **Ship the LLM executive Summary on gene + disease pages, and populate `biobtree_version`.** Together these activate the two differentiators Atlas already *claims* but doesn't *deliver* in its published state: cross-dataset synthesis (Track B #1) and verifiable reproducible provenance (Track B #5). Lowest effort, highest credibility payoff — closes the gap between the marketing thesis and the shipped artifact. *(RAG, Competitive)*

2. **Close the self-referencing cross-entity graph — internal links + Atlas-pointing JSON-LD edges (gene↔disease↔drug).** (Track B #9) This is the single biggest *unrealized* unique asset: the same MONDO/HGNC/ChEMBL IDs already recur across all three entity types, the drug side already proves the pattern, and no public biomedical resource publishes a fully connected dereferenceable graph. It simultaneously fixes the SEO link-equity leak, gives RAG systems a navigable ID-joined graph, and is mostly a renderer change over existing data.

3. **Answer-first synthesis layer: precision-oncology CIViC verdict (low effort) → AlphaMissense×SpliceAI×ClinVar concordance → `claims.jsonld` (atomic records).** (Track B #2, #10, #13) These are the three deterministic syntheses that are *genuinely impossible on any single competitor* and are exactly what AI tools lift and cite. Start with the one-line verdict (trivial), build toward the concordance join and the machine-readable claim records.

---

## SEQUENCED NEXT STEPS

**Phase 1 — Publish-state fixes + cheap wins (days, mostly low effort):**
1. Re-run summary step for gene + disease corpora (Track B #1).
2. Wire real `biobtree_version` + snapshot dates into pipeline (B #5).
3. Add `description:` + templated `seo_title:` frontmatter; normalize casing (A #1).
4. Fix disease Pharmacogenomics "VIP:1" mislabel (B #6); suppress dead GWAS/variant-tier sections and *audit the GWAS routing* (B #7).
5. Round floats, suppress empty/teaser sections, dedupe subcellular-location string (A #4, A #5).
6. Filter HuGE-Navigator GeneRIF boilerplate (B #3).
7. Emit stable section anchors keyed to provenance IDs (B #4).
8. Add answer-first CIViC precision-oncology verdict line to leads (B #2).

**Phase 2 — Theme + presentation (1–2 weeks):**
9. `<details>` collapsibles for long tables; reorder sections "answer-first" (A #7, A #8).
10. Table scroll-wrapper, sticky ToC, sidecar `<link rel=alternate>` hints (A #2, A #3, A #6).
11. Sitemap + robots.txt + catalog `_index` hub pages (B #11).
12. Gate disease repurposing/druggability on disease class; add cohort-confidence routing flag (B #14).
13. Disease definition line from Mondo/Orphanet (B #8); drug MoA from ChEMBL + max_phase fix (B #12 REAL portion).

**Phase 3 — Moat / strategic (multi-week):**
14. Cross-entity link mesh + self-closing Atlas-pointing JSON-LD graph (B #9) — *highest strategic leverage*.
15. CIViC triples into JSON-LD structured layer; per-gene druggability A–E tier.
16. AlphaMissense×SpliceAI×ClinVar concordance join (B #10).
17. `claims.jsonld` atomic-assertion sidecar (B #13), then content-addressed fact fingerprints + per-fact provenance.
18. Synthetic hub / drug-response-matrix / pathway-aggregate pages for long-tail SEO.

**Explicitly deferred (aspirational / needs infra or license):** per-regeneration diff pages; any DrugBank-gated PK/dosing/safety content (link-out only).
